description: Add Step Functions state machine with Polly integration task, wire EventBridge rule to target the state machine instead of the stub SQS queue, and write TDD tests for all new resources.
steps: Step 1: Add new tests to src/CdkBase.Tests/CdkBaseStackTest.cs (TDD - tests first). Add the following test methods: (a) Stack_HasStepFunctionsStateMachine - verify exactly 1 AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine resource exists. (b) Stack_StateMachineHasDefinitionWithPollyTask - find the state machine resource, parse its DefinitionString (which will be a JSON string in the CloudFormation template), and verify it contains a state named 'SynthesizeSpeech' of Type 'Task' with Resource containing 'states:::aws-sdk:polly:synthesizeSpeech' or similar polly integration. (c) Stack_StateMachineHasLoggingEnabled - verify the state machine resource has LoggingConfiguration with level ALL and includeExecutionData true. (d) Stack_EventBridgeRuleTargetsStateMachine - verify the EventBridge rule's Targets array contains an entry with Arn referencing the state machine (not the old SQS queue). (e) Stack_StateMachineRoleHasPollyPermissions - find the IAM role's policies and verify there is a policy statement allowing polly:SynthesizeSpeech action. (f) Stack_StateMachineRoleHasCloudWatchLogsPermissions - verify the execution role policy includes permissions for logs:CreateLogDelivery, logs:GetLogDelivery, logs:UpdateLogDelivery, logs:DeleteLogDelivery, logs:ListLogDeliveries, logs:PutResourcePolicy, logs:DescribeResourcePolicies, logs:DescribeLogGroups. (g) Remove or update the test Stack_StubQueueHasKmsEncryption, Stack_StubQueueHasDeadLetterQueue, Stack_DeadLetterQueueHasKmsEncryption since the stub queues will be removed. Update Stack_EventBridgeRuleHasTarget to still pass (it just checks Targets exists). Update resource counts if needed (remove the 2 SQS queue resources).

Step 2: Implement the Step Functions state machine in src/CdkBase/CdkBaseStack.cs. Add the necessary using statements: Amazon.CDK.AWS.StepFunctions, Amazon.CDK.AWS.Logs, Amazon.CDK.AWS.IAM. Create a CloudWatch Logs log group for state machine execution logs. Create the state machine definition using the CDK StepFunctions L2 constructs: (a) Use a CustomState or define a Pass/Task chain. For the Polly integration, use a CustomState with stateJson that defines an AWS SDK integration task (Type: Task, Resource: arn:aws:states:::aws-sdk:polly:synthesizeSpeech, Parameters with placeholder values for Text, OutputFormat, VoiceId). (b) Chain: Start -> SynthesizeSpeech Task -> End (Succeed state). (c) Create the StateMachine with DefinitionBody.FromChainable(...), logging configuration (level ALL, includeExecutionData true, destination log group), and tracingEnabled true.

Step 3: Wire the EventBridge rule to target the state machine. Remove the stub SQS queues (StubProcessingQueue and StubProcessingDLQ) and the SqsQueue target. Replace with: rule.AddTarget(new SfnStateMachine(stateMachine, new SfnStateMachineProps { ... })). Use Amazon.CDK.AWS.Events.Targets.SfnStateMachine. This passes the event as input to the state machine execution.

Step 4: Add least-privilege IAM permissions for Polly. The state machine execution role needs polly:SynthesizeSpeech permission. CDK may auto-grant some permissions when using SDK integrations, but if using CustomState, add an explicit policy statement: stateMachine.AddToRolePolicy(new PolicyStatement { Actions = ['polly:SynthesizeSpeech'], Resources = ['*'] }).

Step 5: Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. Update the 'Current Implementation Status' section to describe the new Step Functions state machine and Polly integration, and remove references to the stub SQS queue. Update the current-state Mermaid diagram (flowchart LR) to show: Input S3 Bucket -> EventBridge Rule -> Step Functions State Machine -> Amazon Polly. Keep the Output S3 Bucket in the diagram. Add a new subsection 'Orchestration Layer' describing the Step Functions state machine and its role.
acceptance_criteria: The test file contains tests verifying: state machine existence, Polly task in definition, logging configuration, EventBridge targets state machine, IAM role has Polly permissions, IAM role has CloudWatch Logs permissions
src/CdkBase/CdkBaseStack.cs defines a Step Functions state machine with a Polly SynthesizeSpeech task state, CloudWatch logging, and proper IAM role
The EventBridge rule targets the state machine (no more stub SQS queue)
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md is updated with new state machine information and updated Mermaid diagram
No SQS queue resources remain in the stack (stub queues removed)
Code compiles correctly following C# CDK patterns (will be validated by CI)
verification: AST/syntax validation: Review the C# code for correct syntax, proper using statements, and correct CDK API usage
Pattern consistency: Verify the new code follows the same patterns as existing code (internal constructor, L2 constructs, proper namespaces)
Test coverage: Confirm all new infrastructure resources have corresponding test assertions
Documentation: Verify ARCHITECTURE.md is updated and Mermaid diagram is consistent with implementation
findings: Implemented without local build/test verification due to no dotnet SDK in sandbox. Code follows existing patterns exactly. CI pipeline will validate. Used CustomState for Polly task since there is no L2 construct for AWS SDK service integrations. SfnStateMachine target from Amazon.CDK.AWS.Events.Targets used for EventBridge wiring.
